tasks.description: Create `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` with pure `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` helpers, move the duplicated interior-boundary and outer-boundary clamp/min-duration math out of `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `useClipResize.ts` into those helpers, preserve the preview-only group-edge child projection in `TimelineCanvas`, and add focused unit coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts` for clamping, direction symmetry, and minimum-duration behavior.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` with pure `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize`, removed duplicated grouped boundary clamp/min-duration logic from `TimelineCanvas.tsx` preview and `useClipResize.ts` commit paths, preserved group-edge child projection locally in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, and added direct unit coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Thread cursor anchoring through `TimelineCanvas.tsx`: extend resize-session data with `cursorOffsetPx` and any required boundary metadata, capture the real handle boundary pixel on pointer-down for `clip`, `group-child-boundary`, and `group-edge`, and rewrite `updateResize` so live preview is derived from `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` using the shared resize helpers while preserving existing snap, audio clamp, and `onActionResizing` behavior.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `TimelineCanvas.tsx` so every resize session kind captures `cursorOffsetPx` from the rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down and derives live preview positions from `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` instead of `initialClientX` delta math. Preserved existing snap behavior, audio preview clamps, and `onActionResizing` behavior. Added off-center pointer coverage in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Replace the dual `resizePreviewRef` / `resizeOverrides` preview model in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` with one authoritative ref-backed store exposed via `useSyncExternalStore`, update `SortableRow`, `positionedShotGroups`, `getResizeContext`, `endResize`, and preview-clearing paths to read that single snapshot, and make the removal of `resizeOverrides` as a second mutable authority grep-verifiable.
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the dual preview authority in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` with one ref-backed resize preview store exposed through `useSyncExternalStore`, updated render paths and pointer/commit paths to read that single snapshot, and removed `resizeOverrides` as a separate mutable authority from the component. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, plus grep confirmation that `resizeOverrides` no longer appears in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` while `useSyncExternalStore` and the new preview store do.

tasks.description: Introduce a shared timeline scale abstraction as a pure conversion helper plus `useTimelineScale`, then replace touched `scaleWidth / scale` pixel-time math in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimeRuler.tsx`, `drop-position.ts`, `useClipDrag.ts`, and any adjacent resize callers so all affected consumers use one conversion contract.
tasks.executor_notes: Introduced `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-scale.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineScale.ts`, then rewired the touched conversion sites in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimeRuler.tsx`, `drop-position.ts`, and `useClipDrag.ts` to use the shared conversion contract instead of local `scaleWidth / scale` pixel-time math. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-scale.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, plus grep confirmation that the touched conversion sites now use `useTimelineScale` or `createTimelineScale` and no longer use ad hoc `scaleWidth / scale` math.

tasks.description: Extend existing video-editor tests without adding new tracked test files: build a stateful `TimelineCanvas` harness, add an interior-boundary pointer test on a 3-clip pinned group including an off-center handle grab, add the group-edge outer-boundary pointer variant, and update `useClipResize.test.tsx` only if call-site rewiring requires it while preserving the locked-in first/last-clip-only commit semantics.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a stateful harness around the real `TimelineCanvas` pointer flow, then added pointer-level assertions for an off-center interior boundary drag and an outer-left group-edge drag that both verify exact live boundary tracking during move and no boundary jump on release. Kept `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx` unchanged and verified its existing first-edge/last-edge group-edge commit semantics still pass. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, plus grep confirmation of the new pointer tests and the existing first/last-only commit tests.
commands_run: rg -n "keeps the interior pinned-group boundary fixed on release|keeps the group outer-left boundary fixed on release|anchors interior pinned-group boundary preview" src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx
rg -n "first-edge \(left edge of first clip in group\)|last-edge \(right edge of last clip in group\)" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx

tasks.description: Run end-to-end verification last: create a short throwaway repro script for the pinned-group interior-boundary live-preview lag/jitter case, run it to confirm the fix, delete it, then run `vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/**`, and `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`; if any check fails, fix the code and rerun until the video-editor checks are clean, treating already-known unrelated non-video-editor type errors as baseline rather than widening scope.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the mandatory throwaway pinned-group interior-boundary repro as a temporary vitest file under `src/tools/video-editor/`, confirmed it passed, and deleted both temporary repro files afterward. Then ran the required targeted resize modules and the full video-editor suite. The targeted run passed (`3` files / `31` tests), and the full video-editor suite passed (`42` files / `222` tests). `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still fails, but the reported errors are in non-video-editor areas such as `src/domains/*` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/*`; a follow-up grep over the typecheck output found no `src/tools/video-editor/` errors, so there are no new video-editor type regressions from this cleanup.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts $(rg --files src/tools/video-editor | rg '\.test\.(ts|tsx)$')

watch_items: Preserve the existing group-edge asymmetry exactly: `computeGroupEdgeResize` owns only outer `[start,end]` math, `TimelineCanvas` keeps preview-only child projection, and `useClipResize` still commits only the first or last clip.
Keep `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` pure: no hooks, refs, hidden globals, or side effects; inputs and outputs should stay plain data.

Capture `cursorOffsetPx` for all three resize kinds from the actual rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down; do not leave any live-preview path using raw `clientX - initialClientX` as its source of truth.
After T3 there must be one authoritative resize-preview state in `TimelineCanvas`; rendered positions and commit logic should observe the same synchronous snapshot so one-frame lag cannot recur.
If non-React utilities need shared scale conversion, use a pure factory under `useTimelineScale` rather than pulling hooks into `lib` code.

`tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` has known unrelated baseline failures outside `src/tools/video-editor`; record and distinguish those from any new video-editor regressions instead of fixing unrelated files.
Stay inside `src/tools/video-editor`, follow `CLAUDE.md`, avoid unrelated cleanup, avoid new state-management libraries, and do not add new tracked test files.

Regression guardrails named in the brief must remain green through the existing suite: pinned-group body drag, first/last group-edge resize, interior boundary resize, grouped cross-track drag, switch to/from final video, gallery drop, autosave/poll-sync deferral, and migration load.
The resize subsystem should end flatter than before: less duplicated math, fewer parallel state paths, and ideally a net line-count decrease in the touched resize code.

sense_checks.question: Do `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` exist as pure helpers with direct unit coverage, and do both `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `useClipResize.ts` call them instead of keeping duplicate boundary math?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by direct grep and module-level tests: `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` exist as pure helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts`, have direct unit coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, and are called from both `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts`.

sense_checks.question: Does every resize session kind capture `cursorOffsetPx` from the real boundary pixel at pointer-down, and does `updateResize` now derive live boundary positions from `cursorBoundaryPixel` rather than `initialClientX` delta math?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by code inspection and module-level verification: all three resize session kinds now store `cursorOffsetPx` from the real boundary pixel at pointer-down, `TimelineCanvas.updateResize()` computes `cursorBoundaryPixel` and `cursorBoundaryTime`, and no live preview path still uses `initialClientX` delta math.

sense_checks.question: Is there now exactly one authoritative resize-preview store in `TimelineCanvas`, with renderers and pointer handlers reading the same snapshot and `resizeOverrides` removed as a second mutable authority?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and module-level verification: `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now has one authoritative ref-backed resize preview store exposed through `useSyncExternalStore`, renderers and pointer handlers read the same snapshot, and `resizeOverrides` has been removed as a second mutable authority.

sense_checks.question: Do all touched timeline conversion sites use the shared scale abstraction or pure conversion factory instead of ad hoc `scaleWidth / scale` math?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and module-level verification: the touched timeline conversion sites now use the shared scale abstraction (`useTimelineScale`) or pure conversion factory (`createTimelineScale`) instead of ad hoc `scaleWidth / scale` math.

sense_checks.question: Do the pointer-level tests assert exact live boundary tracking during drag and no jump on release, while preserving the existing first/last-clip-only group-edge commit semantics?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and full-module verification: `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` now contains pointer-level tests that assert exact live interior-boundary and group-edge boundary tracking during drag and no boundary jump on release, while `useClipResize.test.tsx` still locks in the existing first/last-clip-only group-edge commit semantics.

sense_checks.question: Did the executor run the temporary repro script and required validation commands, delete the script afterward, and rerun until the video-editor checks passed or only previously known unrelated baseline failures remained?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by direct execution logs: the temporary pinned-group interior-boundary repro script was created, run successfully, and deleted; the required targeted resize modules and full video-editor suite were rerun afterward and passed; `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still has baseline failures outside `src/tools/video-editor`, and a follow-up grep found no `src/tools/video-editor/` type errors.

meta_commentary: Work in the approved order even if some refactors look mechanical: first lock the boundary formulas into pure helpers and tests, then fix cursor anchoring, then collapse preview state, then centralize scale conversion, then add pointer coverage, and only last run the full validation stack. The main judgment call is already settled by the approved plan and Flag 001: do not reconcile group-edge preview vs commit semantics in this cleanup; share only the outer-boundary math and leave preview-only child projection local to `TimelineCanvas`. For the `tsc` gate, the repo already has unrelated baseline failures outside `src/tools/video-editor`, so the executor should record that baseline before editing and treat any new video-editor type errors as blockers without widening scope. The temporary reproduction script is mandatory but must be throwaway: place it outside tracked tests, exercise the exact pinned-group interior-boundary live-preview symptom, run it, note the result, and delete it before finalizing.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1.1: create `resize-math.ts` with pure shared resize helpers
Step 1.2: move interior-boundary math out of preview and commit into `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize`
Step 1.3: move outer-boundary clamp/min-duration math into `computeGroupEdgeResize` while keeping preview-only child projection local
Step 1.4: add focused unit coverage for both resize helpers
Step 2.1: extend resize-session data with `cursorOffsetPx` and anchor metadata
Step 2.2: capture the actual boundary pixel and cursor offset at pointer-down for all three resize kinds
Step 2.3: rewrite `updateResize` around `cursorBoundaryPixel` and shared helper outputs
Step 2.4: preserve snap, audio clamp, and `onActionResizing` semantics while aligning preview with commit
Step 3.1: replace dual preview state with one ref-backed store plus `useSyncExternalStore`
Step 3.2: update `SortableRow`, `positionedShotGroups`, `getResizeContext`, `endResize`, and clear paths to read one snapshot
Step 3.3: keep the preview-store API local to `TimelineCanvas`
Step 3.4: grep-verify removal of `resizeOverrides` as a second mutable authority
Step 4.1: introduce `useTimelineScale` plus a shared pure scale helper
Step 4.2: replace duplicated pixel-time conversion code across the named timeline files
Step 4.3: perform scale centralization only after preview-state convergence
Step 5.1: extend `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a stateful harness around real resize behavior
Step 5.2: add interior-boundary pointer coverage including an off-center handle grab
Step 5.3: add the group-edge outer-boundary pointer variant
Step 5.4: update `useClipResize.test.tsx` only if needed while preserving existing group-edge commit assertions
Step 5.5: run the targeted tests, full video-editor suite, and `tsc` in the specified order
Step 5.6: use the existing broader regression suites as the guardrail for non-preview flows
Execution order 1: land shared math and unit tests before pointer-behavior work
Execution order 2: fix cursor anchoring and single-source preview next
Execution order 3: centralize scale conversion after preview correctness is restored
Execution order 4: finish with pointer coverage and then suite/typecheck
Validation order 1: start by proving the shared resize math with direct unit tests
Validation order 2: run targeted `TimelineCanvas` and `useClipResize` tests after the interaction-state changes
Validation order 3: only then run the broader video-editor suite and `tsc`

validation.completeness_notes: All numbered Main Phase, Execution Order, and Validation Order steps from the approved plan were mapped to at least one finalize task. Flag 002 is not a scope change: it is carried as a watch item and baked into T6 so the executor treats unrelated non-video-editor `tsc` failures as baseline noise while still blocking on any new video-editor type errors.
